statement: In 2025, 26% of German enterprises in the survey scope used artificial-intelligence technologies.
company_size_scope: All enterprises in the Destatis ICT survey scope; employee threshold and EU enterprise definition must be retained
notes: Do not compare directly with Bitkom's 20+ employee survey

statement: In 2025, 23% of German enterprises with 10–49 employees used artificial-intelligence technologies.
company_size_scope: 10-49 employees
notes: EU enterprise definition applies from reporting year 2025

statement: In 2025, 36% of German enterprises with 50–249 employees used artificial-intelligence technologies.
company_size_scope: 50-249 employees
notes: EU enterprise definition applies from reporting year 2025

statement: In 2025, 57% of German enterprises with more than 250 employees used artificial-intelligence technologies.
company_size_scope: >250 employees
notes: Source wording says more than 250; retain exact boundary pending methodology review

statement: Among German enterprises using AI in 2025, 52% used text-mining technology.
company_size_scope: AI-using enterprises in survey scope
notes: Denominator is AI-using enterprises, not all enterprises

statement: Among German enterprises using AI in 2025, 35% used natural-language generation, including programming code.
company_size_scope: AI-using enterprises in survey scope
notes: Denominator is AI-using enterprises

statement: Among German enterprises using AI in 2025, 52% used technology that generates images, video, sound, or audio.
company_size_scope: AI-using enterprises in survey scope
notes: Denominator is AI-using enterprises

statement: Among German enterprises using AI in 2025, 23% reported using machine-learning technology.
company_size_scope: AI-using enterprises in survey scope
notes: Destatis category definitions require methodology review

statement: Among German enterprises using AI in 2025, 27% used AI for workflow automation or decision-making.
company_size_scope: AI-using enterprises in survey scope
notes: Denominator is AI-using enterprises

statement: Among German enterprises using AI in 2025, 6% used AI for autonomous movement of machines.
company_size_scope: AI-using enterprises in survey scope
notes: Denominator is AI-using enterprises

statement: In 2025, 20% of German enterprises in the survey scope had considered using AI technologies but did not yet use them.
company_size_scope: All enterprises in survey scope
notes: Barrier percentages below use this subgroup as denominator

statement: Among enterprises that had considered AI but did not use it, 72% cited missing knowledge.
company_size_scope: Enterprises that considered AI but did not use it
notes: Subgroup denominator; not all enterprises

statement: Among enterprises that had considered AI but did not use it, 62% cited uncertainty about legal consequences.
company_size_scope: Enterprises that considered AI but did not use it
notes: Subgroup denominator

statement: Among enterprises that had considered AI but did not use it, 60% cited data-protection and privacy concerns.
company_size_scope: Enterprises that considered AI but did not use it
notes: Subgroup denominator

statement: Among enterprises that had considered AI but did not use it, 45% cited incompatibility with existing equipment, software, and systems.
company_size_scope: Enterprises that considered AI but did not use it
notes: Subgroup denominator

statement: Among enterprises that had considered AI but did not use it, 44% cited problems with availability or quality of required data.
company_size_scope: Enterprises that considered AI but did not use it
notes: Subgroup denominator

statement: Among enterprises that had considered AI but did not use it, 32% cited costs that were too high.
company_size_scope: Enterprises that considered AI but did not use it
notes: Subgroup denominator

statement: Among enterprises that had considered AI but did not use it, 23% cited ethical considerations.
company_size_scope: Enterprises that considered AI but did not use it
notes: Subgroup denominator

statement: Among enterprises that had considered AI but did not use it, 18% considered AI not useful.
company_size_scope: Enterprises that considered AI but did not use it
notes: Overall value only; large-company value suppressed as statistically uncertain

statement: Germany had 3.2 million small and medium-sized enterprises in the reported 2023 scope.
company_size_scope: EU-aligned SME definition used by Destatis
notes: Scope covers specified WZ 2008 sectors; read methodology before extrapolation

statement: Small and medium-sized enterprises represented 99.3% of enterprises in the reported German 2023 scope.
company_size_scope: EU-aligned SME definition used by Destatis
notes: Scope covers specified WZ 2008 sectors

statement: Small and medium-sized enterprises accounted for 53% of persons employed in enterprises in the reported German 2023 scope.
company_size_scope: EU-aligned SME definition used by Destatis
notes: Confirm exact table denominator during full content review

statement: Small and medium-sized enterprises accounted for 41% of gross value added in the reported German 2023 scope.
company_size_scope: EU-aligned SME definition used by Destatis
notes: Confirm exact table denominator during full content review

statement: Small and medium-sized enterprises accounted for 26% of turnover in the reported German 2023 scope.
company_size_scope: EU-aligned SME definition used by Destatis
notes: Scope covers specified WZ 2008 sectors

statement: In a representative Bitkom survey conducted in weeks 2–6 of 2026, 41% of German companies with at least 20 employees reported using AI.
company_size_scope: >=20 employees
notes: Telephone survey commissioned by Bitkom; population differs from Destatis

statement: In the same 2026 Bitkom survey, 48% of German companies with at least 20 employees reported planning or discussing AI use.
company_size_scope: >=20 employees
notes: Planning and discussion are combined in one response category

statement: Among surveyed companies currently using AI, 77% said their competitive position had improved.
company_size_scope: AI-using companies with >=20 employees
notes: Self-reported outcome; subgroup size not stated on page

statement: Among surveyed companies currently using AI, 52% reported a measurable contribution to company success.
company_size_scope: AI-using companies with >=20 employees
notes: Self-reported; definition of measurable success requires underlying questionnaire

statement: Among surveyed companies currently using AI, 66% intended to expand their use of AI.
company_size_scope: AI-using companies with >=20 employees
notes: Subgroup size not stated on page

statement: In the 2026 Bitkom survey, 61% of companies said they used their data potential little or not at all.
company_size_scope: >=20 employees
notes: 48% little plus 13% not at all; self-assessment

statement: Among surveyed AI-using companies, 33% reported substantially higher AI costs than expected.
company_size_scope: AI-using companies with >=20 employees
notes: Confirm question wording and subgroup denominator from downloadable presentation

statement: The AI Act rules on AI literacy in Article 4 have applied since 2 February 2025.
company_size_scope: Providers and deployers of AI systems
notes: Regulatory information; route binding interpretation to authoritative law/courts

statement: AI-literacy measures should reflect the organization's provider/deployer role, the system's risk and context, and users' prior knowledge and experience.
company_size_scope: Organizations providing or deploying AI systems
notes: Guidance is context-specific, not one-size-fits-all

statement: The AI Act does not prescribe standardized training measures for AI literacy.
company_size_scope: Organizations providing or deploying AI systems
notes: Absence of a standardized format does not remove the underlying duty

statement: Bundesnetzagentur recommends documenting measures taken to build AI literacy.
company_size_scope: Organizations providing or deploying AI systems
notes: Recommended records include type, content/duration scope, and participants

statement: Certification of AI-literacy training or qualification measures is not required by the AI Act.
company_size_scope: Organizations providing or deploying AI systems
notes: Internal or external measures may be used

statement: The European Commission's current framework page says the AI Act becomes fully applicable on 2 August 2026, subject to listed exceptions and later high-risk-system dates.
company_size_scope: AI Act actors
notes: Volatile: active simplification agreement changes parts of the timeline

statement: The Bundesnetzagentur's current overview page states that the AI Act will be fully applicable from August 2027.
company_size_scope: AI Act actors
notes: Conflicts in wording with the Commission page; preserve both and obtain legal/current-status review before product output
